content:
type: SPEC
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
spec: In NDCG.compute(), add a new optional parameter: relevance_scores: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None (place after retrieved_texts, before **kwargs; import Dict is already imported at top of file). Docstring: add a line for it, e.g. 'relevance_scores (Optional[Dict[str, float]]): Optional mapping from expected_id to a float relevance grade. If not provided, binary relevance (1.0 if in expected_ids else 0.0) is used, preserving current behavior.' Behavior change (only touches the two sum(...) blocks, does not touch your ideal_length/count fix - apply this on top of whatever ideal_length logic you use): 1) Define a helper to get a doc's relevance: rel = relevance_scores.get(docid, 1.0) if docid in expected_set else 0.0  -- when relevance_scores is None, keep exactly the current expression (docid in expected_set) so dtype/behavior for binary case is byte-identical. 2) DCG sum: for each retrieved docid, call discounted_gain(rel=<that relevance value>, i=i, mode=mode) same as now, just swap in the graded relevance lookup when relevance_scores is not None. 3) IDCG (ideal) sum: this must also use graded values when relevance_scores is provided, since ideal gain must reflect the best possible ranking by relevance, not just presence. Build ideal_relevances = sorted((relevance_scores.get(eid, 1.0) for eid in expected_ids), reverse=True) when relevance_scores is not None, else keep the current rel=True path. Then iterate i in range(1, ideal_length+1) (using your ideal_length/count, whatever you decide it to be) and call discounted_gain(rel=ideal_relevances[i-1], i=i, mode=mode) instead of rel=True, guarding against index errors if ideal_length exceeds len(ideal_relevances) (it shouldn't if ideal_length <= len(expected_ids), just want that invariant preserved). 4) No other behavior changes when relevance_scores is None -- output must be identical to before your IDCG fix in the binary case. 5) Also update the class docstring line 'Currently only supports binary relevance...' to note that graded relevance is now supported via relevance_scores.
note: relevance_scores dict maps expected_id -> float grade; affects both DCG and IDCG computation
